sql_array: CREATE TABLE example_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data XML, description VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO example_data (id, data, description) VALUES (1, '<root><element>Sample</element></root>', 'Sample entry'), (2, '<root><element>Test</element></root>', 'Test entry'), (3, '<root><element>Edge</element></root>', 'Edge case entry with long description exceeding the usual length limit'), (4, NULL, 'Entry with NULL data');
SELECT xml_view_dtd((SELECT data FROM example_data WHERE description NOT LIKE '%NULL%')) AS dtd_output;
SELECT xml_view_dtd((SELECT data FROM example_data WHERE data IS NOT NULL AND description NOT LIKE 'Sample%')) AS filtered_dtd;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM example_data WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM example_data sub WHERE sub.id = example_data.id AND sub.description NOT LIKE 'Edge%');
SELECT data FROM example_data WHERE description NOT LIKE (SELECT 'Sample%' UNION SELECT 'Test%') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM example_data WHERE data IS NOT NULL);
SELECT xml_view_dtd( (SELECT data FROM example_data WHERE description IS NOT NULL GROUP BY description HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) ) AS grouped_dtd;
SELECT xml_view_dtd( (SELECT data FROM example_data WHERE LENGTH(data) < 200) ) AS short_data_dtd;
SELECT xml_view_dtd('<root>' || (SELECT STRING_AGG(data::VARCHAR, '') FROM example_data WHERE description NOT LIKE '%entry%') || '</root>') AS concatenated_dtd;
